{"signals":[{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.91,"matched_text":"Pruning your Mint plant is a great idea!","distilled_fact":"Pruning a Mint plant is a great idea."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.9,"matched_text":"Pruning is an essential part of maintaining a healthy and bushy Mint plant.","distilled_fact":"Pruning is an essential part of maintaining a healthy and bushy Mint plant."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.89,"matched_text":"Pruning your Mint plant helps:

1. **Control legginess:** Pruning encourages bushy growth and prevents the plant from becoming leggy and sprawling.","distilled_fact":"Pruning a Mint plant encourages bushy growth and prevents the plant from becoming leggy and sprawling."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.89,"matched_text":"Pruning your Mint plant helps:

2. **Promote healthy growth:** Pruning removes dead or damaged leaves and stems, allowing the plant to focus its energy on healthy growth.","distilled_fact":"Pruning a Mint plant removes dead or damaged leaves and stems and allows the plant to focus its energy on healthy growth."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.88,"matched_text":"Pruning your Mint plant helps:

3. **Increase yields:** Pruning stimulates the plant to produce more leaves, which means more fresh Mint for you to enjoy!","distilled_fact":"Pruning a Mint plant stimulates the plant to produce more leaves."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.92,"matched_text":"Cut back long stems: Using clean scissors or pruning shears, cut back long stems to about one-third to one-half of their height.","distilled_fact":"A Mint plant's long stems should be cut back to about one-third to one-half of their height using clean scissors or pruning shears."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.9,"matched_text":"Remove dead or damaged leaves: Remove any dead, damaged, or diseased leaves or stems to prevent the spread of disease and encourage healthy growth.","distilled_fact":"Dead, damaged, or diseased leaves or stems should be removed from a Mint plant to prevent the spread of disease and encourage healthy growth."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.9,"matched_text":"Pinch off flower buds: Pinch off flower buds as they form to direct the plant's energy towards leaf growth instead of seed production.","distilled_fact":"Flower buds should be pinched off from a Mint plant as they form to direct the plant's energy towards leaf growth instead of seed production."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.9,"matched_text":"Prune your Mint plant regularly to maintain its shape and encourage bushy growth. You can prune as often as every 1-2 weeks during the growing season.","distilled_fact":"A Mint plant can be pruned as often as every 1-2 weeks during the growing season."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.84,"matched_text":"Prune in the morning: Prune your Mint plant in the morning, when the oils in the leaves are most concentrated.","distilled_fact":"A Mint plant should be pruned in the morning when the oils in the leaves are most concentrated."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.84,"matched_text":"Use clean tools: Use clean scissors or pruning shears to prevent spreading diseases from one plant to another.","distilled_fact":"Clean scissors or pruning shears should be used to prune a Mint plant to prevent spreading diseases from one plant to another."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.83,"matched_text":"Make clean cuts: Make clean cuts just above a node (where a leaf meets the stem) to promote healthy growth.","distilled_fact":"Clean cuts should be made just above a node where a leaf meets the stem when pruning a Mint plant."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.83,"matched_text":"Don't over-prune: Prune only what's necessary to maintain the plant's shape and promote healthy growth.","distilled_fact":"Only what is necessary should be pruned from a Mint plant to maintain its shape and promote healthy growth."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"plant_care_advice","confidence":0.81,"matched_text":"Over-pruning can stress the plant and lead to disease or pest issues.","distilled_fact":"Over-pruning a Mint plant can stress the plant and lead to disease or pest issues."}]}
